title: 10 best herbal teas for nasal congestion

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus tea helps with nasal congestion because it contains eucalyptus oil, which has natural anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. These properties help to reduce swelling in the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, eucalyptus oil helps to loosen and expel mucus, further reducing congestion. Furthermore, the warm temperature of the tea can help to soothe inflamed membranes in the nose and throat, providing almost instant relief. Moreover, drinking eucalyptus tea can also help to hydrate the body, which is important for maintaining healthy nasal passages and preventing further congestion.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the eucalyptus plant are commonly used in teas to help alleviate nasal congestion due to their anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties.
Bark: Eucalyptus bark is often used in teas to help clear up nasal congestion as it contains compounds with mucolytic and anti-inflammatory effects.
Buds: Eucalyptus buds are rich in aromatic compounds, making them a popular addition to teas for nasal congestion, as they can help to clear the airways and promote easy breathing.

Flowers: The flowers of the eucalyptus plant are a less common, but still effective addition to teas for nasal congestion, as they contain natural oils with anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties.
remedy_constituents: Eucalyptol: This compound has a strong camphor-like smell and acts as a natural decongestant by helping to thin and narrow mucus secretions in the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe.
Tannins: These astringent compounds can help reduce inflammation and swelling in the nasal passages, providing relief from congestion.
Flavonoids: These antioxidant compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation and swelling in the nasal passages, reducing congestion.
Terpinene: This compound has natural antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation and combat infections that may contribute to nasal congestion.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 1 cup of water and 1 tablespoon of dried eucalyptus leaves, then bring to a boil.
Reduce heat to low, cover, and let simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Sip the tea while still warm, allowing the vapors to help clear nasal congestion.

remedies.remedy_desc: Peppermint tea helps with nasal congestion because it contains menthol, a natural decongestant that thins mucus and helps to reduce inflammation in the nasal passages. The warm temperature of the tea also helps to soothe inflammation and open up the airways, providing temporary relief from congestion. Additionally, peppermint has antibacterial and antiviral properties, which can help to fight off the underlying causes of congestion. Drinking peppermint tea can also help to stay hydrated, which is important for maintaining healthy mucous membranes and preventing further irritation. Furthermore, inhaling the steam from the tea can help to loosen up mucus and clear out blocked nasal passages, providing additional relief from congestion.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the peppermint plant contain menthol, which helps to open up nasal passages and alleviate congestion.
Flowers: The flowers of the peppermint plant have essential oils that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the nasal passages and provide relief from congestion.

Seeds: The seeds of the peppermint plant contain rosmarinic acid, which has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them useful in reducing inflammation and congestion in the nasal passages.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: Acts as a natural decongestant and helps to thin mucus, making it easier to remove mucus from the nose and throat.
Rosmarinic acid: Has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the nasal passages, providing relief from congestion.
Limonene: Helps to open up airways and has been shown to provide relief from nasal congestion.
Menthone: Another natural decongestant that helps to relieve nasal congestion and improve breathing.
Pinene: Has expectorant properties that help to loosen up phlegm and mucus, making it easier to expel.
remedy_recipe: In a teapot, add boiling water (1 cup) to dried peppermint leaves (1 teaspoon).
Let the mixture steep for 5-10 minutes to allow the menthol in the peppermint to be released.
Strain the tea to remove the leaves, then pour the liquid into a cup.
Optional: Add honey (to taste) for sweetness and added soothing properties.
Sip the tea slowly, allowing the vapors to enter your nostrils and help clear your nasal congestion.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginger tea helps with nasal congestion because it contains properties that reduce inflammation and irritation in the nasal passages. The main active component in ginger, gingerol, has natural antiviral and antibacterial properties that can help fight infections causing congestion. Furthermore, ginger acts as a natural pain reliever and fever reducer, which can alleviate the discomfort associated with clogged nasal passages. Additionally, ginger can help soothe sore throats and coughs, often accompanying nasal congestion. Lastly, ginger tea can promote perspiration, helping the body to eliminate toxins and further clear nasal congestion.
remedy_parts: Rhizomes: These horizontal, underground stems contain the highest concentration of active compounds, such as gingerols and shaogals, which have natural anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce nasal congestion.
Leaves: Rich in beneficial compounds, ginger leaves may help alleviate sinus pressure and congestion by acting as a natural antihistamine and expectorant.
Buds: Ginger buds contain high levels of essential oils that have been shown to provide relief from nasal congestion by reducing inflammation and promoting mucus membrane health.
Flowers: While not commonly used for medicinal tea, ginger flowers may provide mild relief from nasal congestion due to their natural antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: Gingerols, the most abundant compound in ginger, have potent anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the nasal passages, alleviating congestion.
Shogaols: Shogaols, formed when ginger is dried or cooked, act as natural decongestants by shrinking inflamed blood vessels in the nasal passages.
Zingerone: Zingerone, a compound found in smaller amounts, has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to soothe irritated nasal tissues and clear congestion.

Parsley Oil: Though not a constituent of raw ginger, parsley oil (a common ginger pairing) has been shown to have decongestant properties, further enhancing ginger's soothing effects on nasal congestion.
remedy_recipe: Peel and grate 1 inch of fresh ginger, let it steep in hot water for 5-10 minutes.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey and 1/2 lemon juice for added vitamin C and soothing properties.
Strain the ginger pieces from the tea for easy drinking.
Optional: add a cinnamon stick for added flavor and warmness.
Sip and enjoy the soothing effects of the ginger tea, repeat as needed for nasal congestion relief.

remedies.remedy_desc: Turmeric tea helps with nasal congestion because it contains curcumin, a bioactive substance with strong anti-inflammatory properties. This compound helps reduce nasal swelling and inflammation, providing relief for congestion. Additionally, turmeric's natural decongestant properties help drain the sinuses, alleviating the discomfort and pressure associated with clogged nasal passages. The warmth of the tea also helps to soothe irritated throat membranes and increase nasal airflow. Furthermore, turmeric's antimicrobial and antiviral properties may help protect against infections that can cause or worsen nasal congestion, thus promoting overall respiratory health.
remedy_parts: Rhizomes: The underground stem of turmeric, rich in curcumin, helps reduce inflammation and relieve nasal congestion.
Leaves: Packed with antioxidants, turmeric leaves may help alleviate symptoms of nasal congestion by reducing inflammation and fighting infections.
Flowers: High in anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, turmeric flowers can help clear nasal passages and fight off the cause of congestion.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: Reduces inflammation in the nasal passages, helping to alleviate congestion.
Turmerone: Helps to improve breathing by reducing swelling in the nasal passages.
Demethoxycurcumin: Has anti-inflammatory properties that can help relieve symptoms of nasal congestion.
Bisdemethoxycurcumin: Helps to break down mucus, making it easier to expel and reducing nasal congestion.
Essential oil: Contains anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can help to reduce nasal congestion and fight off infections.
remedy_recipe: In a saucepan, combine 2 cups of water and 1 teaspoon of grated fresh turmeric (or 1/2 teaspoon of powered turmeric), bring to a boil.
Strain the tea into a cup, add honey and lemon to taste.
Optional: sprinkle a pinch of black pepper to increase turmeric's bioavailability.
Sip and enjoy the soothing effects of this tea, have 2-3 cups a day for nasal congestion relief.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea tea helps with nasal congestion because it contains several active compounds, such as cichoric acid, echinacoside, and polysaccharides, which have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immune-boosting properties. These compounds work together to reduce inflammation in the nasal passages, helping to alleviate congestion. Moreover, echinacea has been shown to stimulate the immune system, enabling the body to fight off infections that can cause nasal congestion. Additionally, echinacea may help to decrease the production of mucus in the nasal passages, further reducing congestion and promoting easier breathing. Ultimately, the combined effects of echinacea on inflammation, immunity, and mucus production make it a valuable tool in managing nasal congestion.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in antioxidants, the flowers of echinacea help reduce inflammation in the nasal passages, easing congestion.
Leaves: High in tannins, the leaves of echinacea have astringent properties, helping to tighten mucous membranes and relieve nasal congestion.
Roots: Packed with immune-boosting compounds, the roots of echinacea offer powerful relief for nasal congestion by stimulating the immune system.
Rhyzomes: Containing rosmarinic acid, the rhyzomes of echinacea have anti-inflammatory effects, helping to alleviate nasal congestion caused by inflammation.
remedy_constituents: Phenols: These compounds in echinacea have antioxidant properties that can help reduce inflammation and congestion in the nasal passages.
Alkamides: These active constituents have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and immune-stimulating effects, which can help clear up nasal congestion.
Essential oils: The aromatic oils in echinacea, such as caryophyllene and humulene, have been found to have decongestant and anti-inflammatory effects.
Polysaccharides: These complex carbohydrates have been shown to stimulate the immune system, which can help the body fight off infections that cause nasal congestion.
Glycoproteins: These proteins have been found to have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce swelling and congestion in the nasal passages.
Vitamins and minerals: Echinacea contains several vitamins and minerals, including vitamin C, vitamin E, and zinc, which can support immune function and help reduce inflammation.
remedy_recipe: In a saucepan, combine 2 cups of water and 2 tablespoons of dried echinacea flowers and leaves.
Bring the water to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 15 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the echinacea fragments, then add honey and lemon to taste.
Let the tea cool until it's warm, but not hot, to avoid burning your nasal passages.
Sip the tea slowly, allowing it to coat your throat and nasal passages for relief.

remedies.remedy_desc: Chamomile tea helps with nasal congestion because it contains anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling in the nasal passages, enabling easier breathing. Its soothing effects can also help alleviate irritation in the throat, which often accompanies congestion. Furthermore, chamomile is known for its antioxidant and antimicrobial properties, which can help boost the immune system and fight off infections causing congestion. The warm temperature of the tea can additionally help to thin and drain mucus, providing additional relief. Lastly, chamomile tea is a natural alternative to over-the-counter medication, making it a gentle and safe option for both children and adults.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Chamomile flowers are used to make medicinal tea for nasal congestion due to their anti-inflammatory properties which help reduce swelling in the nasal passages.
Leaves: The leaves of chamomile contain antioxidants that help boost the immune system, making it easier to fight off conditions that cause nasal congestion.
Seeds: Chamomile seeds are rich in flavonoids that have antispasmodic properties, helping to relax the muscles that cause coughing and narrowing of the airways, thus alleviating nasal congestion.
remedy_constituents: Apigenin: Acts as a natural antihistamine, helping to reduce inflammation and congestion in the nasal passages.
Luteolin: Possesses anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate nasal congestion by reducing swelling in the nasal passages.
Chamazulene: Has anti-inflammatory and anti-spasmodic effects, helping to relax muscles around the nasal passages and relieve congestion.
Bisabolol: Known for its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, it helps reduce irritation and inflammation in the nasal passages.
remedy_recipe: Boil 8 ounces of water and let it cool for 1-2 minutes.
Pour the hot water over 1-2 teaspoons of dried chamomile flowers.
Sip and enjoy the soothing effects of the warm tea, up to three times a day.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba tea helps with nasal congestion because it contains flavonoids and terpenoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help reduce swelling in the nasal passages. These compounds work to improve blood circulation, enabling the body to fight off infections and reduce congestion more effectively. Furthermore, ginkgo biloba has been shown to inhibit the release of histamine, a compound that contributes to inflammation and nasal congestion. Additionally, its natural anti-inflammatory effects can help alleviate symptoms of allergies, including runny nose and sinus pressure. Drinking ginkgo biloba tea may provide relief for individuals experiencing nasal congestion due to colds, flu, or allergies, making it a natural and beneficial alternative to over-the-counter medications.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the ginkgo biloba plant are commonly used in teas due to their high concentration of flavonoids and terpenoids, which can help reduce inflammation and improve breathing by alleviating nasal congestion.
Seeds: Ginkgo biloba seeds contain compounds that can help decrease inflammation and act as a natural expectorant, making them beneficial for tea intended to alleviate nasal congestion.
Bark: The bark of the ginkgo biloba tree has been used in traditional medicine for its antioxidant properties, which can help soothe inflamed nasal passages and promote better breathing.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These antioxidant compounds help reduce inflammation in the nasal passages, helping to alleviate congestion.
Terpene lactones: The terpene lactone called bilobalide can help improve blood flow, which may reduce nasal congestion by increasing oxygen supply to the nasal tissues.
Ginkgolides: Ginkgolides, particularly ginkgolide B, have anti-inflammatory properties that can help minimize nasal inflammation and congestion.
remedy_recipe: Boil 8 ounces of water to a rolling boil, which typically takes 3-5 minutes on a stove.
Add 1 teaspoon of dried Ginkgo Biloba leaves to the boiling water, then reduce heat and simmer for 5 minutes.
Remove the pot from heat and let the tea steep for an additional 10 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the leaves, then let it cool down to a drinkable temperature.
Sip the Ginkgo Biloba tea slowly, allowing it to soothe and potentially help clear nasal congestion.

remedies.remedy_desc: Elderberry tea helps with nasal congestion because it contains bioflavonoids, particularly anthocyanins, that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These compounds help reduce swelling in the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, elderberry has been found to help boost the immune system, which can help the body fight off infections that may be causing congestion. The tea's warm temperature also works to soothe and relax the respiratory system, providing almost immediate relief for stuffy noses. Furthermore, elderberry tea is a natural remedy, making it a safer and healthier alternative to over-the-counter decongestants, which can have harsh side effects.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in bioflavonoids and antioxidants, elderberry flowers can help reduce nasal congestion by alleviating inflammation and supporting the immune system.
Berries: High in vitamins A, B, and C, as well as flavonoids, elderberries can help clear nasal congestion by reducing inflammation and fighting off infections.
Leaves: Containing tannins and flavonoids, elderberry leaves can help soothe mucous membranes and provide anti-catarrhal properties, making it useful for relieving nasal congestion.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Elderberry flavonoids, such as quercetin, have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling in the nasal passages, alleviating congestion.
Anthocyanins: These powerful antioxidants found in elderberries help boost the immune system, which can help the body fight off infections causing nasal congestion.
Vitamin C: Elderberries are a natural source of vitamin C, which is known to support the immune system and can help reduce inflammation, thereby alleviating nasal congestion.
Tannins: Elderberry tannins have astringent properties that can help shrink and tighten the nasal lining, providing relief from congestion.

Volatile oils: The volatile oils in elderberries have been found to have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce inflammation and fight off infections causing nasal congestion.
remedy_recipe: In a saucepan, combine 1 cup of water and 1-2 tablespoons of dried elderberries; bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce heat, cover, and let it simmer for 15-20 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the elderberries, then discard the solids.
Add honey and lemon juice to taste, if desired, and stir well.

remedies.remedy_desc: Licorice tea helps with nasal congestion because it contains a compound called glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. This compound helps to reduce swelling in the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, licorice tea can help to soothe a sore throat and reduce coughing, further alleviating symptoms of congestion. Furthermore, the warm temperature of the tea can help to thin and loosen mucus, making it easier to expel. However, it is important to note that licorice tea should be consumed in moderation, as excessive consumption can lead to side effects such as high blood pressure and potassium depletion. Therefore, it is always best to consult with a healthcare provider before starting any new herbal remedy.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant are used to make medicinal tea for nasal congestion because they contain glycyrrhizin, which helps to reduce inflammation and soothe irritation in the respiratory system.
Flowers: The flowers of the licorice plant are used in medicinal tea for nasal congestion as they have expectorant properties, which help to clear phlegm and mucus from the respiratory tract.
Seeds: Licorice seeds are used in medicinal tea for nasal congestion as they act as an antitussive, helping to alleviate coughs and soothe sore throats.

Bark: The bark of the licorice plant is used in medicinal tea for nasal congestion because it has expectorant and demulcent properties, which help to calm irritation and reduce inflammation in the respiratory system.
remedy_constituents: Liquiritin: This flavonoid has been shown to have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce swelling in the nasal passages and relieve congestion.
Liquiritigenin: This compound found in licorice has been found to have antiviral properties, which can help fight off viral infections that may cause nasal congestion.
Glycyrrhizin: This triterpenoid saponin has expectorant properties, which can help loosen and expel mucus from the respiratory tract, relieving nasal congestion.
Isoliquiritigenin: This flavonoid found in licorice has been shown to have anti-allergic effects, which can help alleviate nasal congestion caused by allergies.
Glabridin: This isoflavane has anti-inflammatory properties and can help reduce swelling and irritation in the nasal passages, providing relief from congestion.
remedy_recipe: In a teapot, combine 1 teaspoon of crushed licorice root with 8 ounces of boiling water.
Steep the mixture, covered, for 10-15 minutes to allow the beneficial compounds to infuse.
Strain the tea to remove the licorice pieces, preventing over-steeping that may cause bitterness.
Add honey or lemon to taste, if desired, for enhanced flavor and soothing effects.
Sip the warm tea gradually, allowing it to coat your throat and relieve nasal congestion.

remedies.remedy_desc: Marshmallow tea helps with nasal congestion because it contains mucilage, a substance that becomes slippery when it comes into contact with water. This slippery substance can effectively coat and soothe inflamed mucous membranes, reducing irritation and swelling. As a result, the airways become less congested, making it easier to breathe. Moreover, marshmallow tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can further alleviate inflammation in the nasal passages, providing additional relief from congestion. Drinking marshmallow tea can also help increase hydration, which is essential for thinning and loosening mucus, allowing it to drain more easily from the nose. Ultimately, marshmallow tea's soothing, anti-inflammatory, and hydrating effects can provide natural and effective relief from nasal congestion.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, marshmallow flowers can help soothe irritated membranes and reduce inflammation in the nasal passages.
Leaves: The soothing mucilage in marshmallow leaves can help relieve irritation and congestion by coating and lubricating the mucous membranes in the nose.
Roots: Marshmallow roots contain high levels of mucilage, which can help alleviate nasal congestion by reducing inflammation and providing a protective barrier in the respiratory tract.
Stems: The stems of marshmallow plants also contain mucilage, which can help ease congestion, soothe irritation, and promote healthy mucus flow in the nasal passages.
remedy_constituents: Mucilage: The mucilage in marshmallow coats and soothes irritated mucous membranes, providing relief from nasal congestion.
Polysaccharides: The polysaccharides found in marshmallow have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation and swelling in the nasal passages.
Flavonoids: Marshmallow flavonoids have been found to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, reducing the symptoms of nasal congestion.
Phenolic Acids: The phenolic acids in marshmallow have been shown to have antibacterial and antiviral properties, helping to fight infections that can cause nasal congestion.
remedy_recipe: Boil 1 cup of water and steep 1 teaspoon of dried elderflowers for 10 minutes; strain out the flowers.
Add 2-3 marshmallows (preferably homemade and free of artificial ingredients) to the hot elderflower tea.
Gently stir the tea and marshmallows until the marshmallows are completely melted.
Let the marshmallow tea cool to a comfortable drinking temperature.
Sip the marshmallow tea slowly, allowing the soothing, mucus-thinning properties to alleviate nasal congestion.
